display.purpose: The DUPLiiST wordmark only — gradient-filled, 800 weight, -2px tracking.

headline.purpose: Playlist names, modal titles, drawer header.

body.purpose: Input text, track titles, descriptions; razor-legible system sans.

shadows.name: focus-ring
shadows.purpose: Soft violet halo on focused inputs — never a hard outline.

shadows.name: glow-primary-rest
shadows.purpose: Violet gradient buttons at rest, lit from within.

shadows.name: glow-primary-hover
shadows.purpose: Primary button on hover — glow widens, button lifts.

shadows.name: glow-action
shadows.purpose: Ghost action button hover glow, color-matched to its role accent.

shadows.name: bloom-success
shadows.purpose: Cyan celebratory bloom fired once when a track reaches 'downloaded'.

shadows.name: splash-lift
shadows.purpose: The only dark shadow in the system — seats the cinematic splash card, always paired with violet.

motion.name: ease-fluid
motion.purpose: Exponential ease-out for fluid entrances and the splash sequence.

motion.name: ease-spring
motion.purpose: Controlled spring for state-change pops (status pills, toggles, toast).

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Violet-gradient action button (Search / Save) with a resting glow that intensifies and lifts on hover; presses with a spring scale.
components.html: <button class="ds-btn-primary">Buscar <svg width="15" height="15" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2.5"><path d="M5 12h14M12 5l7 7-7 7"/></svg></button>

components.name: Download Action Button
components.description: Ghost action button — transparent fill, color-coded border (cyan = download), faint accent fill and matched glow on hover. Disabled state drops to 35% with no glow.
components.html: <button class="ds-btn-download"><svg width="13" height="13" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2"><path d="M21 15v4a2 2 0 0 1-2 2H5a2 2 0 0 1-2-2v-4"/><polyline points="7 10 12 15 17 10"/><line x1="12" y1="15" x2="12" y2="3"/></svg>Descargar</button>

components.name: Search Input
components.description: The primary URL / query field. Translucent dark fill, faint border, violet focus halo with a faint violet fill tint. Placeholder is muted-deep.

components.name: Glass Card
components.description: The signature container — violet-tinted glass over a 24px blur, 16px radius, glow-border, and a luminous violet hairline along the top edge.
components.html:
Playlist cargada

Superficie de cristal, iluminada desde dentro.

components.name: Status Pill
components.description: The instrument readout of the deck. A color-coded pill naming a track's state; 'found' is amethyst-on-violet-dim, 'downloaded' adds a cyan success bloom. Pops with a spring on every change.
components.html: ✓ Encontrado ⬇ Ya descargado

components.name: Source Pill
components.description: Header 'live' pill in the source brand color with a pulsing currentColor dot. Spotify green shown; YouTube red and SoundCloud orange are siblings.

components.name: Format Toggle Pill
components.description: Format / quality selector pills. Active gets a violet fill + border + amethyst text and a pop-in spring; inactive is muted ghost.

narrative.overview: DUPLiiST is a deep-space premium cockpit for moving music between platforms. The canvas is a near-black void (#06060f); above it drift aurora-grade blooms of electric violet and signal cyan. That light is never wallpaper — it is the instrument panel: every glow, gradient sweep, and pulse reports a track's state, guides the eye, or marks a brand moment. The system is cinematic and alive — the splash is an opening title sequence, rows stagger in, status pills pop the instant they change. Energy is the point, but every animation must resolve to information or attention, never just decoration. It explicitly rejects the generic dev-tool look, cheap gratuitous glow, and any sketchy-downloader-site feel; because it touches credentials and copyrighted content, trust is a visual requirement.
keyCharacteristics: Void-black canvas (#06060f) with drifting aurora violet/cyan blooms behind a precise interface.
Glow is the depth language — colored light bloom and glassmorphism, never flat gray drop shadows.
Glassmorphism cards with a luminous violet hairline along the top edge.
Choreographed, fluid motion: a cinematic splash, staggered rows, spring-popped state changes.
Color-coded status pills make every track state scannable at a glance.
Per-source brand colors (Spotify green, YouTube red, SoundCloud orange) reserved strictly for their platforms.
Single workflow spine: paste → resolve → select → convert/download, kept obvious under the polish.

rules.name: The Glow-Is-Signal Rule
rules.body: Light always reports state or guides attention. If a glow doesn't change with state and you could delete it without losing information, it's decoration — cut it.

rules.name: The Source-Truth Rule
rules.body: Spotify green, YouTube red, and SoundCloud orange are reserved for their platforms. They never become generic UI accents.

rules.name: The Violet-Cyan Polarity Rule
rules.body: Violet is action and identity; cyan is completion. The brand gradient runs violet → cyan because that is the literal journey of the product.

rules.name: The Gradient-Wordmark Rule
rules.body: Gradient text is reserved for the DUPLiiST wordmark and footer mark only — forbidden on headings, body, labels, and buttons.

rules.name: The Tabular-Time Rule
rules.body: All durations and numeric counts use tabular-nums so columns never jitter during live search.

rules.name: The Colored-Light Rule
rules.body: Elevation is a glow matched to the element's own accent. Neutral gray box-shadows are forbidden.

rules.name: The Glass-Surface Rule
rules.body: Primary containers are translucent glass — violet-tinted fill plus blur — so the void shows through. Surfaces are lit, not stacked.

dos: Do keep the void-black canvas (#06060f) and let the aurora violet/cyan blooms breathe behind a precise interface.
Do express all depth as colored glow matched to the element's own accent, plus glassmorphism. Light, not shade.
Do make motion fluid and cinematic — ease-out cubic-bezier(.16,1,.3,1) for entrances, spring cubic-bezier(.34,1.56,.64,1) for state pops — and keep the splash super smooth.
Do give every track state a color-coded status pill, and keep the paste → resolve → select → convert/download spine obvious under the polish.
Do reserve Spotify green, YouTube red, and SoundCloud orange for their platforms only.
Do confine gradient text to the DUPLiiST wordmark and footer mark.
Do add a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) fallback for every animation — this is currently missing and must be closed before ship.
Do keep display letter-spacing at or above -0.04em.
donts: Don't ship the generic dev-tool look — bare forms, default browser styling, no motion, no personality.
Don't add gratuitous gradients or glows that don't serve hierarchy or feedback. Every glow must report state or guide attention.
Don't let anything read as a sketchy/dodgy downloader site — no aggressive ads, no dark patterns, no confusing CTAs.
Don't use gradient text on any heading, label, body, or button — wordmark only.
Don't introduce flat gray drop shadows. If it looks like a 2014 app, the shadow is gray and the glow is missing.
Don't use border-left/border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe — cards carry their accent as a full glow-border plus top hairline.
Don't rely on muted-deep (#475569) or muted (#64748b) for anything that must be read — small legal/placeholder copy at .71rem on the void fails the 4.5:1 contrast floor.
